name: Piri-Piri Chinese Hot Sauce
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat the sherry in a large skillet over medium heat. Add the Piri-piri chiles (with seeds) and fry until browned, about 5 minutes.
2. Add the minced garlic and continue to cook for another 2-3 minutes, stirring occasionally.
3. Stir in the soy sauce, powdered ginger, sugar, and sesame oil. Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer for 5-10 minutes, or until slightly thickened.
4. Strain the hot sauce through a fine-mesh sieve into a clean container, discarding the solids.
5. Allow the sauce to cool completely before storing in an airtight container in the refrigerator.
I felt like a chemist making the stuff. I placed it on a back burner under the vent and stood back while stirring it gently. After tasting the sauce by dipping a spoon into it and letting the sauce drain off, then taking a small taste by placing the spoon on the tip of my tongue, nothing happened at first. After about 15 seconds or so, the heat kicked in. The sauce tasted pretty good.
